records.ety: [LL. capitulatus, p. p. of capitulare to capitulate: cf. F. capituler. See Capitular, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Capitulated; p. pr. & vb. n. Capitulating.]

defs: To settle or draw up the heads or terms of an agreement, as in chapters or articles; to agree.
quotes: There capitulates with the king . . . to take to wife his daughter Mary.
There is no reason why the reducing of any agreement to certain heads or capitula should not be called to capitulate.

defs: To surrender on terms agreed upon (usually, drawn up under several heads); as, an army or a garrison capitulates.
quotes: The Irish, after holding out a week, capitulated.

defs: To surrender or transfer, as an army or a fortress, on certain conditions.
